pattern: Because the earlier index must hold the smaller value, tracking only the smallest value seen so far while scanning left to right lets every later element be compared against the best possible partner in one pass, instead of checking every pair.
hints: Scan the array once from left to right, keeping a running minimum of everything seen so far.
At each position, if the current value is larger than the running minimum, it is a valid candidate pair -- compute the difference and keep the best one.
Only update the running minimum when the current value is smaller than it, since a valid pair always needs the smaller value to come first.

edge_inputs.description: strictly increasing, best pair is first and last
strictly decreasing, no valid increasing pair exists
the smallest possible array, an increasing pair
the smallest possible array, a decreasing pair
values at both extremes of the allowed range
all values identical, so no strictly increasing pair exists
the smallest value appears late, after a larger value that must not be used as its partner

stress.description: fully random values at the maximum array length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
